title: Japan
subtitle: 62 days · Cape to Cape
body:
It's only after a tour that you realize the depth of the relationship you've cultivated with the land you've traversed.

Many years ago, I stumbled upon 'japancycling.org', a resource outlining potential cycling routes across Japan. The concept bewildered me… "cycling across a country?? Japan?" I was enamored at the prospect, but never felt I'd have the means or time to pursue such an adventure.

Now, I find myself digesting the fact that I had accomplished just that, but with the prospect of many, many more miles ahead.

I began my ride across Japan with a lot of doubt and uncertainty, reeling from the fact that I abandoned the security of a stable job and community. But somewhere along the way I found myself settling into this weird, transient way of life — embracing the uncertainty and gaining confidence in "figuring it out."

Cycling across a country is one of the most intimate ways to experience its land, people, and culture. The exhilaration of exploration, paired with the challenges of survival, creates a bond with a place like no other.

Japan, you have my heart — you'll be dearly missed.

Reasons that make Japan a superior location for bikepacking:
· There is an abundance of cheap or free campsites
· Konbinis and Vending Machines are everywhere making for easy resupply
· Onsens! ♨️
· The drivers are some of the most mindful I've ever encountered
· Coolish™️ Ice Cream!!!
· Cicadas and the heat act as a natural alarm clock 🥴
· Opportunity to explore a new language
